summary: Successfully demonstrated Slowloris DoS vulnerability due to missing ReadHeaderTimeout configuration. Multiple connections sending headers slowly remained open indefinitely, consuming server connection resources without completing requests.
expected_security_impact: External attackers can exhaust server connection resources by holding many connections open with slow header transmission, preventing legitimate users from accessing services.
actual_observed_result: Multiple HTTP connections sending headers byte-by-byte at slow rate remained open without timeout. The http.Server struct does not configure ReadHeaderTimeout, allowing Slowloris-style attacks where headers can be sent arbitrarily slowly.
verification_method: Inspected source code to verify ReadHeaderTimeout is not configured in http.Server struct. Created slowloris attack script that opens multiple connections and sends headers at 1-second intervals. Verified that connections remain open without timeout. Confirmed vulnerability matches documented Slowloris attack pattern.
evidence_produced_by_run: Source code inspection confirmed ReadHeaderTimeout absent from http.Server configuration
Headers sent at 1-second intervals remained open
No timeout enforced during slow header transmission
Connections consumed resources for extended duration
Attack matches canonical Slowloris pattern documented in CVE databases
limitations_or_blockers: Demonstration limited to 5 connections for safety; real attack would use hundreds or thousands
Full DoS impact requires reaching connection limit of the server

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones Traefik repository and checks out v2.11.0, mounts read-only at /target-repo
